features.title: A co-op Ghost survival map
features.body: Play as a small squad of Ghost commandos dropped into a hostile jungle. You survive by moving together, scavenging resources, and adapting before the swarm overwhelms you.

features.title: Craft weapons and shape your role
features.body: Your Ghost is not locked to the default attack. Craft and swap weapons, then upgrade into a sniper, brawler, explosives specialist, support survivor, or hybrid build.

features.title: Progression that feels personal
features.body: Each player upgrades independently: weapon damage, range, fire rate, health, armor, shields, energy, mobility, and utility can all become part of your build.

features.title: A jungle that changes every run
features.body: Resources are placed from randomized jungle candidates, mobs respawn by area, and evolving threats push the squad to explore instead of camping one safe spot.

features.title: Build tactically, then move on
features.body: Temporary structures help you hold ground, recover, or prepare for danger — but everything can be deconstructed when it is time to relocate.

features.title: Readable escalating enemies
features.body: Zerg mobs evolve automatically over time. Their names and colors — Black, Brown, Orange, Red — tell players how dangerous each variant has become.

evolutions.name: Black Forager
evolutions.change: Low HP scout-worker. Mostly tests player aim and reveals where the swarm is spreading.

evolutions.name: Brown Gnawer
evolutions.change: Gains more HP and a faster bite, making ignored worker packs dangerous.

evolutions.name: Orange Stripper
evolutions.change: Gains bonus damage against structures and deconstruct targets, punishing static defenses.

evolutions.name: Red Ravager
evolutions.change: Fast raider with high structure damage and enough HP to slip through weak perimeter fire.

evolutions.name: Black Brood
evolutions.change: Very fragile melee flood unit that appears in numbers.

evolutions.name: Brown Brood
evolutions.change: More movement speed and HP, better at surrounding isolated Ghosts.

evolutions.name: Orange Brood
evolutions.change: Much faster attack cooldown; dangerous if allowed to stack on a player.

evolutions.name: Red Brood
evolutions.change: Tiny berserker variant with high speed and burst damage, forcing quick repositioning.

evolutions.name: Black Skitter
evolutions.change: Basic fast melee enemy: low HP, low damage, short vision.

evolutions.name: Brown Skitter
evolutions.change: Gains +HP and movement speed, becoming reliable chase pressure.

evolutions.name: Orange Ripper
evolutions.change: Switches to a sharper claw profile: higher damage and shorter cooldown.

evolutions.name: Red Ripper
evolutions.change: Elite hunter with max speed, high damage, and enough durability to punish wounded players.

evolutions.name: Black Spitter
evolutions.change: Short-range spine attack with modest damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Spitter
evolutions.change: Gains HP and +1 weapon range, letting it fire from behind melee mobs.

evolutions.name: Orange Needler
evolutions.change: Swaps to a faster needle weapon with better cooldown and higher damage.

evolutions.name: Red Needler
evolutions.change: Long-range elite shooter with high damage, forcing players to break line of sight or snipe it first.

evolutions.name: Black Burrower
evolutions.change: Slow setup ambusher with low damage spikes.

evolutions.name: Brown Burrower
evolutions.change: More HP and stronger spine damage, holding narrow paths better.

evolutions.name: Orange Impaler
evolutions.change: Longer spine range and higher splash pressure, threatening grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Impaler
evolutions.change: Elite area-control unit with very long reach and lethal line damage if not cleared quickly.

evolutions.name: Black Matron
evolutions.change: Light support threat with low HP and short-range harassment.

evolutions.name: Brown Matron
evolutions.change: Gains HP and faster movement so it can stay behind the front line.

evolutions.name: Orange Hexer
evolutions.change: Switches to a longer-range disruption weapon/effect to pressure priority targets.

evolutions.name: Red Hexer
evolutions.change: High-priority support unit with long range and punishing debuff-style pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Wing
evolutions.change: Light air threat with low HP and short-range poke.

evolutions.name: Brown Wing
evolutions.change: Gains movement speed and HP, making it harder to kite.

evolutions.name: Orange Razorwing
evolutions.change: Upgraded bouncing glaive damage for harassment against grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Razorwing
evolutions.change: Fast elite flyer with higher damage and longer range, demanding anti-air weapon swaps.

evolutions.name: Black Siegewing
evolutions.change: Slow air artillery with low damage but long enough range to teach target priority.

evolutions.name: Brown Siegewing
evolutions.change: Gains HP and stronger siege damage against structures.

evolutions.name: Orange Shellwing
evolutions.change: Longer range and higher impact damage, forcing the squad to move or counter-snipe.

evolutions.name: Red Shellwing
evolutions.change: Elite siege threat with very long range and heavy structure/player pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Bloater
evolutions.change: Slow fragile special unit with a small death warning.

evolutions.name: Brown Bloater
evolutions.change: Gains HP and a larger death burst radius.

evolutions.name: Orange Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Death effect becomes plague-style damage over an area, punishing close-range kills.

evolutions.name: Red Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Large plague/explosion death effect that can break grouped players if killed carelessly.

evolutions.name: Black Crusher
evolutions.change: Slow tank with high HP but low early damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Crusher
evolutions.change: Gains armor and a larger HP pool, requiring sustained fire.

evolutions.name: Orange Mauler
evolutions.change: Gains speed and a stronger melee weapon, becoming a real chase threat.

evolutions.name: Red Mauler
evolutions.change: Boss-like elite with heavy armor, huge HP, and punishing melee damage.

upgrades.title: Crafted weapons
items: Swap away from the default Ghost attack into crafted weapons.
Shotgun: short range burst / possible splash.
Sniper: long range, slower cooldown, high damage.
Flamer or acid weapon: area control concept.
Explosive weapon: anti-pack option with splash tradeoffs.

upgrades.title: Weapon upgrades
items: Weapon behavior swaps by crafted weapon type

gameplayLoop.title: Survive five nights in the jungle
gameplayLoop.body: Victory comes after surviving five nights in the jungle. The current target is a five-night run, potentially ending with a final boss before extraction or victory. Endless mode can layer on later for players who want to keep pushing.

gameplayLoop.title: Scavenge by day, endure the night
gameplayLoop.body: Pressure comes in waves instead of constant attacks. Days give players room to scout, harvest, expand, and prepare. Nights are more intense, with heavier mob pressure and a greater need for light, teamwork, and smart positioning.

gameplayLoop.title: Control one Ghost, not an army
gameplayLoop.body: Each player controls a single Ghost hero and must micro it carefully. The camera is free by default, with optional camera follow for players who want it. Movement is unconstrained: the whole jungle is open.

gameplayLoop.title: Harvest everything into minerals
gameplayLoop.body: Trees, bushes, rocks, crystals, and biological resources can all be harvested by right-clicking. Harvesting converts everything into one readable resource: minerals. While harvesting, your Ghost is committed to the job and cannot fire at the same time.

gameplayLoop.title: Craft one active weapon at a time
gameplayLoop.body: Players craft weapons that replace the default Ghost attack. You can only equip one weapon at a time, but crafted weapons remain available, can be swapped back to, and can be upgraded over the run.

gameplayLoop.title: Build safe pockets, then abandon them
gameplayLoop.body: Light sources gradually brighten the nearby screen and prevent mob spawning in their area. Walls, regeneration stations, and turrets help the squad hold ground — but death destroys your built structures, and the map pressures you to move.

gameplayLoop.title: Death hurts, but progression survives
gameplayLoop.body: When a Ghost dies, the player respawns at a random position. They lose most carried resources and all structures they built, but keep upgrades and unlocked progression. The penalty is serious without forcing players out of the game.

gameplayLoop.title: The swarm evolves with time
gameplayLoop.body: Enemy evolution is mainly time-based, but player actions like heavy killing or deep exploration can accelerate the threat. The map scales for 1–5 players, rewarding collaboration because the squad is stronger together.
